text:
(a) In this section, “converter” means a person who holds a converter’s license issued under Chapter 2301 (Sale or Lease of Motor Vehicles) , Occupations Code.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach metal converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(c) The fee for a metal converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(c) The fee for a converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(d) The department shall prescribe the form of an application under this section.

(e) A fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach metal converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(c) The fee for a metal converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(c) The fee for a converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(d) The department shall prescribe the form of an application under this section.

(e) A fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 503. Dealer's & Mfr.'s Vehicle License Plates
##### § 503.0618 — Converter’s License Plates

(a) In this section, “converter” means a person who holds a converter’s license issued under Chapter 2301 (Sale or Lease of Motor Vehicles) , Occupations Code.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach metal converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(c) The fee for a metal converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(c) The fee for a converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(d) The department shall prescribe the form of an application under this section.

(e) A fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach metal converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(b) Instead of registering under Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) a vehicle that a converter operates or permits to be operated on a public street or highway, the converter may apply for, receive, and attach converter’s license plates to the vehicle if it is the type of vehicle that the converter is engaged in the business of assembling or modifying.

(c) The fee for a metal converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(c) The fee for a converter’s license plate is $20 a year.

(d) The department shall prescribe the form of an application under this section.

(e) A fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 668 (H.B.718), Sec. 13, eff. July 1, 2025.*
